headline: Earth Welcomes a Forest Comeback the Size of Two U.S. States
summary: A new global study reveals that tree cover worldwide has expanded by around 2.2 million km² over the past 35 years — an area as large as Texas and Alaska combined. This growth comes from large-scale planting projects, natural regrowth on abandoned farmland, and stronger forest protection efforts. The unexpected gain highlights humanity’s power to help nature rebound.

headline: Young Hands Plant the Future in Kenya
summary: Schoolchildren across Kenya have planted more than one million trees, part of a campaign to restore forests and fight climate change.

headline: New Hope for Spinal Injury Patients
summary: Swiss researchers developed a bio-gel implant that helped paralyzed patients regain some leg movement in early trials — a breakthrough in regenerative medicine.

headline: Solar Energy Hits New Price Record
summary: The International Energy Agency confirmed that solar power is now the most affordable electricity source in history, with costs falling another 15% this year.

headline: Malaria Deaths Drop Sharply
summary: Global malaria deaths have fallen by 20% over the past decade, thanks to the rollout of vaccines and mosquito net programs across affected regions.

headline: Golden Lion Tamarins Return to Brazil’s Forests
summary: After years of breeding efforts, conservationists have reintroduced the rare golden lion tamarin into protected rainforests in Brazil.
